content:
# Intro - AI coding agent built for the terminal

OpenCode is an AI coding agent that runs in your terminal. It can answer questions, add features, make changes to your codebase, and more.

## Prerequisites
- Node.js 18 or later
- npm, yarn, or pnpm

## Install

### curl (recommended)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh | sh
```

### npm
```bash
npm install -g @opencode-ai/opencode
```

### brew
```bash
brew install opencode
```

### Arch Linux
```bash
yay -S opencode
```

### Chocolatey (Windows)
```bash
choco install opencode
```

### Scoop (Windows)
```bash
scoop bucket add opencode https://github.com/opencode-ai/scoop-bucket.git
scoop install opencode
```

### mise
```bash
mise use -g opencode
```

### Docker
```bash
docker run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/workspace opencodeai/opencode
```

## Configure

Run `/connect` to connect to your AI provider. OpenCode supports 75+ providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, and more.

## Initialize

Run `/init` to initialize OpenCode in your project. This creates an `opencode.json` configuration file.

## Usage

- Ask questions about your codebase using natural language
- Request new features or modifications
- Make changes to files automatically
- Use `/undo` and `/redo` to revert or reapply changes
- Share your session with `/share`
- Customize OpenCode's behavior via `opencode.json`

## Share

Use `/share` to create a shareable link (opncd.ai/s/<id>) of your current session for collaboration.

## Customize

OpenCode is fully configurable via `opencode.json`. You can customize themes, keybindings, models, agents, formatters, permissions, and more.
